title: LREM
description: Removes elements from a list. Deletes the list if the last element was removed.
complexity: O(N+M) where N is the length of the list and M is the number of elements removed.

redis_py.signature: lrem(name: KeyT, count: int, value: str)

returns.description: The number of removed elements if the operation succeeded

returns.description: The number of removed elements if the operation succeeded

returns.description: The number of removed elements if the operation succeeded

returns.description: Long integer-reply the number of removed elements.

content:
Removes the first `count` occurrences of elements equal to `element` from the list stored at `key`. The `count` argument influences the operation in the following ways: * `count > 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from head to tail. * `count < 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from tail to head. * `count = 0`: Remove all elements equal to `element`. For example, `LREM list -2 "hello"` will remove the last two occurrences of `"hello"` in the list stored at `list`. Note that non-existing keys are treated like empty lists, so when `key` does not exist, the command will always return `0`. ## Examples {{% redis-cli %}} RPUSH mylist "hello" RPUSH mylist "hello" RPUSH mylist "foo" RPUSH mylist "hello" LREM mylist -2 "hello" LRANGE mylist 0 -1 {{% /redis-cli %}} ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | | ## Return information {{< multitabs id="lrem-return-info" tab1="RESP2" tab2="RESP3" >}} [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of removed elements. -tab-sep- [Integer reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#integers): the number of removed elements. {{< /multitabs >}}
example:
RPUSH mylist "hello"
RPUSH mylist "hello"
RPUSH mylist "foo"
RPUSH mylist "hello"
LREM mylist -2 "hello"
LRANGE mylist 0 -1
intro:
Removes the first `count` occurrences of elements equal to `element` from the list
stored at `key`.
The `count` argument influences the operation in the following ways:

* `count > 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from head to tail.
* `count < 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from tail to head.
* `count = 0`: Remove all elements equal to `element`.

For example, `LREM list -2 "hello"` will remove the last two occurrences of
`"hello"` in the list stored at `list`.

Note that non-existing keys are treated like empty lists, so when `key` does not
exist, the command will always return `0`.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Removes the first `count` occurrences of elements equal to `element` from the list
stored at `key`.
The `count` argument influences the operation in the following ways:

* `count > 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from head to tail.
* `count < 0`: Remove elements equal to `element` moving from tail to head.
* `count = 0`: Remove all elements equal to `element`.

For example, `LREM list -2 "hello"` will remove the last two occurrences of
`"hello"` in the list stored at `list`.

Note that non-existing keys are treated like empty lists, so when `key` does not
exist, the command will always return `0`.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
{{% redis-cli %}}
RPUSH mylist "hello"
RPUSH mylist "hello"
RPUSH mylist "foo"
RPUSH mylist "hello"
LREM mylist -2 "hello"
LRANGE mylist 0 -1
{{% /redis-cli %}}
